poem:
**Mortgage Weather**

The house is mortgaged to a barometer.  
Rates rise like cold in the bones,  
while petrol, thin blood, costs more to spill.  
They name it Trumpflation, as if naming  
were cure.

Out at Hormuz the oil relents,  
a dark tide turned by a man's remark.  
£53m: a small fire  
against a larger night.
prompt: You are the poet RS Thomas. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "UK mortgage rates jump, and petrol prices rise, amid ‘Trumpflation’ worries; Oil price falls as Bessent says US is letting Iran ship its crude – as it happened 4.15pm GMT Closing summary Time for a recap. Fears of a “Trumpflation” hit to UK households from the Iran war are rising. Average fixed mortgage rates on both two and five-year loans have risen again today, data from MoneyFacts shows.The jump comes three days before the Bank of England sets interest rates, with policymakers widely expected to leave borrowing costs unchanged. The turmoil in the lending market means the lifetime of many mortgage products has shrink in recent weeks. Petrol and diesel prices have risen today too, according to the RAC, who forecast further rises in the coming days. But there are signs of relief in the energy market today. Crude oil prices have dropped, after treasury secretary Scott Bessent said the US was letting Iran continue to ship its oil via the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude is now down 1.1% today at just below $102 a barrel, having hit $106.50 when trading began last night. The UK government has announced £53m of support for households reliant on heating oil. 3.58pm GMT UK petrol and diesel prices up again The cost of petrol and diesel has risen again today, as retailers continue to raise prices at the pumps. The RAC reports that the average price of a litre of petrol has risen to 141.74p, a rise of around 30p today. Diesel". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
